description: Validate rotational damping for spinning rigid bodies using explicit angular velocity, angular damping labels, rotation trace, and monotonic spin-decay evidence.
physical_assumptions: Spin is a rigid-body angular velocity state, not a visual-only rotation animation.
The spinning object must declare initial angular velocity and angular damping.
Angular speed should decay over time when damping is positive.
The object should accumulate observable rotation while spin decays.
Angular speed gain requires an explicit external torque or it is a failure.
verifier_rules: Initial angular velocity and angular damping labels are present.
Trajectory contains angular_velocity_deg_s for the spinning body.
Angular speed decreases by at least the expected amount.
Final angular speed stays below the expected upper bound.
Angular speed does not increase without an external torque label.
Rotation delta is large enough to prove the body actually spun.
repair_suggestions: Move initial angular velocity into `initial_angular_velocity_deg_s` and expected_physics labels.
Set angular damping as structured physics control rather than prompt prose.
Export angular_velocity_deg_s and rotation_deg in the runtime trajectory.
Add an external torque label before allowing spin speed to increase.
